skillName: Generate linear sequences using term to term rules
misconceptionSummary: Students apply the rule to the starting term only once or change the rule halfway through the sequence.

steps.narration: This sequence is wrong because the rule changes at the end.
The last step does not follow the rule.
The correct next term should be seventeen.
Now the whole sequence follows the rule consistently.

visuals.expression: Check every gap against the rule
visuals.label: Consistency matters in generated sequences

steps.narration: To avoid the mistake, verify that every step uses the same term-to-term rule.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: losing the rule after two or three terms. Check every consecutive pair to make sure the pattern stays consistent.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Do not assume the sequence is correct just because the first few terms work. Check all the steps.
